Don’t wait until the last moment to submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods  (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Student
This visa allows you to stay and study in Belgium for more than 90 days. To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least 4 weeks before the intended date of departure. 
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Student
This visa allows you to stay and study in Belgium for more than 90 days. To avoid any disappointment, applicants are advised to submit their complete files at least 4 weeks before the intended date of departure. 
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Studying  (More than 90 days) Belgium
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use  coloured photo with plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not  tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
Don’t wait until the last moment to submit your visa application 
The decision making time might be longer during some periods  (summer months, holiday season). Therefore, make sure to submit your visa early enough.
Submit a complete file 
Read attentively the information about the necessary documents to be provided before submitting your file. If the Consulate needs to ask for additional documents before being able to take a decision or before sending your file to the Immigration Office, you might not receive your visa on time.

## Download Forms

 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visit to Family or Friends
Applicants traveling to Belgium to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa. 

In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visit to Family or Friends
Applicants traveling to Belgium to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa. 

In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Family Visit or Private Invitation(Belgium)
Family Visit or Private Invitation(Luxembourg)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days at the Embassy. This can be extended to 30 calendar days in case of missing documents or further investigation by the Embassy and can be extended in case the file has to be submitted to the Immigration Office. For further information regarding the processing time, please consult their webpage.

## Download Forms
NOTE: Applicants should  bring photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated  requirements in a standard format.
 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. If during any portion of their visit to Belgium the applicant intends to engage in business, or official duties he/she must refer to other relevant visa categories.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. If during any portion of their visit to Belgium the applicant intends to engage in business, or official duties he/she must refer to other relevant visa categories.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Tourism  (Belgium)
Tourism (Luxembourg)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days at the Embassy. This can be extended to 30 calendar days in case of missing documents or further investigation by the Embassy and can be extended in case the file has to be submitted to the Immigration Office. For further information regarding the processing time, please consult their webpage.

## Download Forms
- You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family Reunion For Children
(In case that your mother or father is Belgian citizen) or (In case that your mother or father to be joined is spouse or partner of Belgian citizen)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Family  Reunion for Children with One Belgian Parent

NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The maximum legal processing time for  a long stay visa D is 6, 9 or 12 months from the date the application  is not only submitted but also completed.

## Download Forms

 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.name: Accredited Travel Insurance
visa_types.overview:
The overseas travel medical insurance required for Schengen visa (visa C) and valid for all Schengen-countries has to cover the applicant for at least 30,000 Euros or equivalent, for all risks e.g. accident, illness, medical emergency evacuation, repatriation of mortals remains, etc.
The policy has to clearly specify the period of validity and has to cover the entire duration of the trip including the date of arrival & departure.
Only overseas travel medical insurance issued by the Philippine insurance companies listed here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/sites/default/files/content/list_of_schengen_insurance_companies_20_october_2022.pdf) are accepted.
visa_types.full_text:
The overseas travel medical insurance required for Schengen visa (visa C) and valid for all Schengen-countries has to cover the applicant for at least 30,000 Euros or equivalent, for all risks e.g. accident, illness, medical emergency evacuation, repatriation of mortals remains, etc.
The policy has to clearly specify the period of validity and has to cover the entire duration of the trip including the date of arrival & departure.
Only overseas travel medical insurance issued by the Philippine insurance companies listed here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/sites/default/files/content/list_of_schengen_insurance_companies_20_october_2022.pdf) are accepted.

visa_types.name: Visa Types

SHORT STAY (#shortstayvisac)
- Tourism
- Family       Visit or Private Invitation
- Student
- Seafarers
- Cohabitation

LONG STAY (#longstayvisad)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Requirements for Visa for Family Reunification with EU Citizen 
The visa for family reunification with BE citizen (ART 40) is given in request for a Long Stay Visa D in order to join husband/wife or father/mother who is a national of a country of the European Union and who lives in Belgium. As a prior requirement, documents submitted must be legalized first. 

Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Requirements for Visa for Family Reunification with EU Citizen 
The visa for family reunification with BE citizen (ART 40) is given in request for a Long Stay Visa D in order to join husband/wife or father/mother who is a national of a country of the European Union and who lives in Belgium. As a prior requirement, documents submitted must be legalized first. 

Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Family  Reunion with Belgium Spouse

NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The maximum legal processing time for  a long stay visa D is 6, 9 or 12 months from the date the application  is not only submitted but also completed.

## Download Forms

 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Directive 2004/38/EC on the right of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States
These rules don’t apply for Belgian citizens living/residing in Belgium if they never lived for minimum 3 months in another country of the EU before returning to Belgium.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Directive 2004/38/EC on the right of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States
These rules don’t apply for Belgian citizens living/residing in Belgium if they never lived for minimum 3 months in another country of the EU before returning to Belgium.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required

 Visa for Family members of EU Citizens (visa C)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use coloured photo with plain white background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair behind ears).
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.

## Download Forms

You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/).

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to study in  Belgium you must apply for student visa. For more information on student visa  application, please click here (http://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/visa/studying-belgium). 
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Credit Card
Where to pay the Contribution Fee?
SPF Intérieur, Office des étrangers, boulevard Pacheco 44, 1000 Bruxelles :
IBAN : BE57 6792 0060 9235
BIC : PCHQBEBB
Banque : BPOST SA, Centrum Monnaie (sans numéro), 1000 Bruxelles
The communication of the transfer must make it possible to identify the applicant. In principle, it takes the name, first name, date of birth and nationality of the applicant, respecting the following structure: Last name First name Nationality Birthdate (DDMMYYYY) (eg: FISCH Elena Philippines 28021981).

## Documents Required
Student (Belgium)
Student (Luxembourg)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days.

## Download Forms
 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The Visa for cohabitation in Belgium allows you to enter Belgium with a National Visa (180 days) and while in Belgium, sign with your partner a legal cohabitation contract and then apply for a residence permit.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Long Stay visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Long Stay visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The Visa for cohabitation in Belgium allows you to enter Belgium with a National Visa (180 days) and while in Belgium, sign with your partner a legal cohabitation contract and then apply for a residence permit.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Long Stay visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Long Stay visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Cohabitation (Belgium)
Cohabitation (Luxembourg)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days at the Embassy. This can be extended to 30 calendar days in case of missing documents or further investigation by the Embassy and can be extended in case the file has to be submitted to the Immigration Office. For further information regarding the processing time, please consult their webpage.

## Download Forms

You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business visa is required  if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or  commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc;  such visas can only be issued to travellers not being paid for work by an  entity within Belgium and its representing countries. If the applicant intends  to undertake paid work in Belgium and its representing countries, he/she will  need to obtain a work visa 
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business visa is required  if during any portion of the visit applicant intends to conduct business or  commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc;  such visas can only be issued to travellers not being paid for work by an  entity within Belgium and its representing countries. If the applicant intends  to undertake paid work in Belgium and its representing countries, he/she will  need to obtain a work visa 
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Only seamen who are nationals from a country which is required to have a valid visa to enter the Schengen space need to apply for a seamen transit visa and if they intend to join a ship that is (or will be) in a Schengen port, land with the aim of joining another ship that is arriving (or will arrive) in a Schengen port, or land due to sickness, repatriation, holiday and so on.
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.

Overview
Marriage
The visa is for Filipino and Foreign nationals resident in the Philippines who intend to get married to Belgian Nationals in Belgium
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Long Stay visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Long Stay visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Marriage
The visa is for Filipino and Foreign nationals resident in the Philippines who intend to get married to Belgian Nationals in Belgium
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Long Stay visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Long Stay visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.
Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Marriage (Belgium)
Marriage (Luxembourg)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new  photo format, as explained below:
Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The legal processing time for a Schengen visa is 15 calendar days at the Embassy. This can be extended to 30 calendar days in case of missing documents or further investigation by the Embassy and can be extended in case the file has to be submitted to the Immigration Office (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/EN/Pages/home.aspx). For further information regarding the processing time, please consult their webpage (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/EN/Application-guides/Pages/Processing%20time%20for%20a%20visa%20application.aspx).

## Download Forms

 You must fill out your visa application form by clicking here. (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/Account/Login?ReturnUrl=%2Fen%2FVisaApplication%2F)

## Overview
Family Reunion With Non-Eu Spouse (Art. 10&10bis)
Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to take a live digital photo.

## Visa Fees
Please click here (https://philippines.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/consular-services/tariff) to find out more about the Visa fees
Aside from visa fee, there is a VFS service fee of Php 2,060 payable online upon booking an appointment.

Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/phl/en/bel/premium-services) to find out more about the VFS  additional services
*Note that Visa fees and VFS additional services are payable in Cash and Card

## Documents Required
Family  Reunion with Non EU Spouse (Belgium)
NOTE: Applicants should bring colored photocopies of all their supporting documents as per the stated requirements in a standard format.

## Photo Specifications

Applicants, submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 

## General
- The       photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil       Aviation Organization). 
- Use        coloured photo with plain white background.
- The        photo must be no more than 6-months old (not used in any visas).
- Not        tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly (hair        behind ears).
- Close        up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80%        of the photograph.
- Photo        in sharp focus and clear.

## Attaching  the Photo to the Application form
- Glue       one photograph on the designated space of the application form.

## Processing Time
The maximum legal processing time for  a long stay visa D is 6, 9 or 12 months from the date the application  is not only submitted but also completed.

## Download Forms

visa_types.name: LONG STAY VISA D
visa_types.overview:
- The Visa D is applicable if you wish to remain a long time (more than 90  days) or to establish yourself definitively in Belgium.
- The Visa D also allows you to travel within the entire Schengen area.
- As of March 2015, the processing of some visa D applications will  require payment of a contribution to be deposited directly in a bank account in  Belgium prior to the submission of the application file. Carefully read the information
HERE (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/EN/Application-guides/Pages/Administrative%20fee.aspx)
.
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register to take a live digital photo
visa_types.full_text:
- The Visa D is applicable if you wish to remain a long time (more than 90  days) or to establish yourself definitively in Belgium.
- The Visa D also allows you to travel within the entire Schengen area.
- As of March 2015, the processing of some visa D applications will  require payment of a contribution to be deposited directly in a bank account in  Belgium prior to the submission of the application file. Carefully read the information
HERE (https://dofi.ibz.be/sites/dvzoe/EN/Application-guides/Pages/Administrative%20fee.aspx)
.
- Applicants applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register to take a live digital photo

visa_types.name: SHORT STAY VISA C
visa_types.overview:
- A Schengen visa normally allows you to travel within the entire Schengen  area.
- The Schengen Member States are: Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic,  Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy,  Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg,  Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain,  Sweden and Switzerland.
- A Schengen visa might allow you a duration of no more than 90 days  (short stay) in any 180 day period.
- The Embassy of Belgium in Manila shall be competent for examining and  deciding on your visa application if:
- Belgium constitutes  the sole destination of the visit(s).
- if the visit includes more than one  destination, Belgium constitutes the main destination of the visit(s)  in terms of the length or purpose of stay.
- if no main destination can be determined,  Belgium constitutes the first point of entry of the territory of the Member  States.

Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
- This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
visa_types.full_text:
- A Schengen visa normally allows you to travel within the entire Schengen  area.
- The Schengen Member States are: Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic,  Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy,  Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg,  Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain,  Sweden and Switzerland.
- A Schengen visa might allow you a duration of no more than 90 days  (short stay) in any 180 day period.
- The Embassy of Belgium in Manila shall be competent for examining and  deciding on your visa application if:
- Belgium constitutes  the sole destination of the visit(s).
- if the visit includes more than one  destination, Belgium constitutes the main destination of the visit(s)  in terms of the length or purpose of stay.
- if no main destination can be determined,  Belgium constitutes the first point of entry of the territory of the Member  States.

Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The applicant is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the applicant and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
- This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the applicant to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.
